channel.title: Whamcloud Community JIRA
channel.description: This file is an XML representation of an issue

item.title: [LU-2398] "maximum lustre stack" messages on PPC client in memory reclaim paths
item.description:
While running an IOR on Vulcan I see a single Lustre client (IO node) with the following messages in its log:

2012-11-27 14:42:32.932604 {DefaultControlEventListener} [mmcs]{52}.5.3: cannot allocate a tage (0)
2012-11-27 14:42:32.933545 {DefaultControlEventListener} [mmcs]{52}.5.3: Lustre: 3793:0:(file.c:2880:ll_layout_refresh()) maximum lustre stack 12368
2012-11-27 14:42:32.934066 {DefaultControlEventListener} [mmcs]{52}.5.3: Call Trace:
2012-11-27 14:42:32.934568 {DefaultControlEventListener} [mmcs]{52}.5.3: [c0000003e4e10f00] [c000000000008160] .show_stack+0x7c/0x184 (unreliable)
2012-11-27 14:42:32.934997 {DefaultControlEventListener} [mmcs]{52}.5.3: [c0000003e4e10fb0] [800000000694e518] .ll_layout_refresh+0x1418/0x1cb0 [lustre]
2012-11-27 14:42:32.935436 {DefaultControlEventListener} [mmcs]{52}.5.3: [c0000003e4e11180] [80000000069cde7c] .vvp_io_init+0x25c/0x370 [lustre]
2012-11-27 14:42:32.935941 {DefaultControlEventListener} [mmcs]{52}.5.3: [c0000003e4e11240] [800000000249b4a4] .cl_io_init0+0x104/0x260 [obdclass]
2012-11-27 14:42:32.936453 {DefaultControlEventListener} [mmcs]{52}.5.3: [c0000003e4e112f0] [80000000045f9e50] .osc_lru_shrink+0x540/0x1390 [osc]
2012-11-27 14:42:32.936972 {DefaultControlEventListener} [mmcs]{52}.5.3: [c0000003e4e11490] [80000000045fb14c] .osc_lru_del+0x3cc/0x760 [osc]
2012-11-27 14:42:32.937522 {DefaultControlEventListener} [mmcs]{52}.5.3: [c0000003e4e115c0] [80000000045fcb30] .osc_page_delete+0x150/0x550 [osc]
2012-11-27 14:42:32.938101 {DefaultControlEventListener} [mmcs]{52}.5.3: [c0000003e4e11690] [800000000248a540] .cl_page_delete0+0x150/0x820 [obdclass]
2012-11-27 14:42:32.938606 {DefaultControlEventListener} [mmcs]{52}.5.3: [c0000003e4e11770] [800000000248ac94] .cl_page_delete+0x84/0x250 [obdclass]
2012-11-27 14:42:32.939292 {DefaultControlEventListener} [mmcs]{52}.5.3: [c0000003e4e11820] [80000000069b17a0] .ll_releasepage+0x170/0x1b0 [lustre]
2012-11-27 14:42:32.939924 {DefaultControlEventListener} [mmcs]{52}.5.3: [c0000003e4e118d0] [c0000000000932dc] .try_to_release_page+0x68/0x8c
2012-11-27 14:42:32.940426 {DefaultControlEventListener} [mmcs]{52}.5.3: [c0000003e4e11940] [c0000000000a3440] .shrink_page_list.clone.0+0x3b0/0x614
2012-11-27 14:42:32.940985 {DefaultControlEventListener} [mmcs]{52}.5.3: [c0000003e4e11af0] [c0000000000a3ab0] .shrink_inactive_list+0x40c/0x71c
2012-11-27 14:42:32.941743 {DefaultControlEventListener} [mmcs]{52}.5.3: [c0000003e4e11cd0] [c0000000000a42d0] .shrink_zone+0x510/0x514
2012-11-27 14:42:32.942242 {DefaultControlEventListener} [mmcs]{52}.5.3: [c0000003e4e11e00] [c0000000000a4dc0] .try_to_free_pages+0x204/0x3d0

2012-11-27 14:42:32.943289 {DefaultControlEventListener} [mmcs]{52}.5.3: [c0000003e4e120e0] [c0000000000c917c] .__slab_alloc+0x1f4/0x68c
2012-11-27 14:42:32.943707 {DefaultControlEventListener} [mmcs]{52}.5.3: [c0000003e4e121b0] [c0000000000ca1c8] .__kmalloc+0xd4/0x150
2012-11-27 14:42:32.944275 {DefaultControlEventListener} [mmcs]{52}.5.3: [c0000003e4e12260] [8000000000aa2504] .cfs_alloc+0x34/0x80 [libcfs]
2012-11-27 14:42:32.944761 {DefaultControlEventListener} [mmcs]{52}.5.3: [c0000003e4e122f0] [8000000003a31fdc] .ptlrpc_request_alloc_internal+0x12c/0x550 [ptlrpc]

2012-11-27 14:42:32.949181 {DefaultControlEventListener} [mmcs]{52}.5.3: [c0000003e4e12fe0] [800000000516c438] .lov_page_cache_add+0xc8/0x340 [lov]
2012-11-27 14:42:32.949654 {DefaultControlEventListener} [mmcs]{52}.5.3: [c0000003e4e130b0] [8000000002486a58] .cl_page_cache_add+0xe8/0x3b0 [obdclass]

2012-11-27 14:42:33.045854 {DefaultControlEventListener} [mmcs]{52}.5.3: [c0000003e4e12a00] [8000000004617ddc] .osc_enter_cache+0x79c/0x12c0 [osc]
2012-11-27 14:42:33.046343 {DefaultControlEventListener} [mmcs]{52}.5.3: [c0000003e4e12bd0] [800000000461fb70] .osc_queue_async_io+0xd10/0x1a40 [osc]

These all look troubling as we've run into stack overrun issues in the past.
item.summary: "maximum lustre stack" messages on PPC client in memory reclaim paths

status.description: A resolution has been taken, and it is awaiting verification by reporter. From here issues are either reopened, or are closed.

comments.comment: patch is at: http://review.whamcloud.com/4701
cl_io_init() in osc_lru_shrink() should not refresh layout, this is easy to fix.
